title: Screen-Space Shadows (Contact Shadows)
description: Covers screen-space contact shadows as a cheap ray-march technique that traces short rays in screen space against the depth buffer to recover high-frequency contact shadow detail that the shadow map's texel resolution cannot capture, applied in a layer on top of shadow maps for near-contact regions. Contact shadows significantly improve the appearance of objects sitting on surfaces and are a standard post-process in every major engine's shadow pipeline. Students will implement screen-space shadow ray marching using Hi-Z acceleration, integrate it as an additive mask over the shadow map result, and tune the maximum ray length to stay within the depth buffer validity region.
